headline: Schwab and Robinhood Stocks Get a Boost on SpaceX IPO Day
summary: Shares of several retail brokers that offered shares of SpaceX to customers at the IPO price rose on Friday, although not as dramatically as SpaceX's more than 20% gain.  Charles Schwab shares were up 3% in late afternoon trading Friday, although the company also had some non-SpaceX good news of its own that probably helped boost the stock: solid growth in new assets and brokerage accounts.  Robinhood's stock gained more than 1.5%, despite the fact that the firm reported some technical issues earlier Friday due to the "record-breaking traffic."

headline: SpaceX IPO Went Off Mostly Smoothly For Retail Brokerages
summary: Investors may not have gotten as many shares of SpaceX from brokerage firms as they would have liked.  There were some technical issues at Robinhood, however.  A spokeswoman for Charles Schwab said that "the process went as anticipated, with an unprecedented number of clients requesting shares and all eligible clients who completed the affirmation process receiving at least a portion of their requested order."

headline: Retail Investors Celebrate—and Complain About—SpaceX Allocations
summary: It seems that some retail traders angling for shares of SpaceX at the $135 IPO price were successful in their quest.  Traders on a Reddit board about Fidelity, one of the five brokerages to offer investors a chance to put in requests for an allocation of SpaceX shares, shared both good and bad stories about the process.  Fidelity lowered its minimum balance requirement from $500,000 for most IPOs to $2,000 to make shares more available.

Fidelity lowered its minimum balance requirement from $500,000 for most IPOs to $2,000 to make shares more available.

headline: SpaceX guide: Everything you need to know about the biggest IPO in history
summary: Investing.com -- SpaceX begins trading on Nasdaq today under the ticker SPCX, targeting a valuation of approximately $1.75 trillion and seeking to raise $75 billion, more than 2.5 times the previous record set by Saudi Aramco (TADAWUL:2222) in 2019. The scale alone rewrites the record books. But the more interesting argument for "unique" runs deeper than the headline number.

The scale alone rewrites the record books. But the more interesting argument for "unique" runs deeper than the headline number.

headline: Why retail investors want in on the SpaceX IPO despite valuation risks
summary: SpaceX is expected to go public at one of the richest IPO valuations in market history, with retail investors set to receive unusual access through platforms like Robinhood and Charles Schwab. CNBC spoke with five retail traders and investors about why they requested SpaceX IPO shares despite concerns over valuation, volatility, Elon Musk, Starlink, AI infrastructure, launch services and whether public-market buyers could become exit liquidity for early investors.

headline: SpaceX IPO: Here's what retails investors need to know
summary: Retail brokerages are lining up to offer SpaceX shares to retail investors who are salivating to buy day one of trade. However, any investor who is thinking of flipping the stock and making a quick buck may want to think twice. Fidelity and Charles Schwab have implemented anti-flipping policies ahead of the historic IPO. CNBC's Seema Mody analyzed other high-profile IPOs, and explains what is unique about SpaceX, to find out what to expect.
